ogTitle: Altona North vs Amherst: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Altona North and Amherst.
heading: Altona North vs Amherst — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Altona North, Victoria and Amherst, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Altona North has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Amherst (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Altona North or Amherst safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Altona North has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Amherst scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Altona North scores 15/100 for bushfire risk. Amherst scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Altona North or Amherst?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Altona North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
